title: Ensure 'Authentication type' is set to 'Azure Active Directory'  only for Azure VPN Gateway point-to-site configuration

input_row.title: Ensure 'Authentication type' is set to 'Azure Active Directory'  only for Azure VPN Gateway point-to-site configuration
input_row.description: Enable only 'Azure Active Directory' (Microsoft Entra ID) authentication for Azure VPN Gateway point-to-site connections.
input_row.rationale: Microsoft Entra ID authentication provides strong security and centralized identity management, and reduces risks associated with static credentials and certificate management.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Virtual network gateways. 2. Under VPN gateway, click VPN gateways. 3. Click the name of a VPN gateway. 4. Under Settings, click Point-to-site configuration. 5. Ensure Authentication type is set to Azure Active Directory only. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each VPN gateway. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 21a6bc25-125e-4d13-b82d-2e19b7208ab7 - Name: 'VPN gateways should use only Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) authentication for point-to-site users'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Virtual network gateways. 2. Under VPN gateway, click VPN gateways. 3. Click the name of a VPN gateway. 4. Under Settings, click Point-to-site configuration. 5. Ensure Authentication type click to expand the drop-down menu. 6. Check the box next to Azure Active Directory, and uncheck the boxes next to Azure certificate and RADIUS authentication. 7. Provide a Tenant, Audience, and Issuer for the Azure Active Directory configuration. 8. Click Save. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each VPN gateway requiring remediation.
input_row.impact: Azure VPN Gateways incur hourly charges, with additional costs for point-to-site connections and data transfer. Pricing varies by SKU and usage. Refer to https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/vpn-gateway/ for details.
input_row.default_value: 'Authentication type' is selected during creation of point-to-site configuration.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Navigate to the AWS Management Console.** 
   a. Go to the Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) dashboard.
   
2. **Locate the VPN Connections.** 
   a. In the navigation pane, click on "VPN Connections."
   b. Identify the VPN connection you wish to evaluate.

3. **Review Client Configuration.** 
   a. Click on the name of the VPN connection.
   b. Select the option for "Client Configuration" to review the point-to-site settings.

4. **Verify Authentication Type.** 
   a. Check the "Authentication" section for the authentication type configured for the VPN.
   b. Ensure that the authentication settings indicate "AWS Client VPN with Active Directory" if that is the desired configuration.

5. **Confirmation of Compliance.** 
   a. If the correct authentication type is set, note that it complies with the control.
   b. If the authentication type is not set as desired, document which configurations need to be modified to ensure compliance.

6. **Repeat for each VPN Gateway and Connection.** 
   a. Go back to the VPN Connections list and repeat the checks for any additional VPN connections that require verification. 

**Expected secure configuration:** 
The expected configuration for the authentication type should align with organizational security policies, ideally set to utilize identity management best practices, such as through AWS Client VPN incorporating authentication measures like Active Directory or similar solutions.

**How to confirm compliance:** 
If the authentication type is correctly configured as per the organizational requirements, it confirms compliance. If discrepancies exist, ensure to document the necessary changes required for remediation.
